Title: An miR156-regulated nucleobase-ascorbate transporter 2 confers cadmium tolerance via enhanced anti-oxidative capacity in barley

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Glutathione; phytochelatins; reactive oxygen species-scavenging antioxidant metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: thiol antioxidant; peptide chelator; redox-related metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidative defense, heavy-metal detoxification, and cadmium tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of HvNAT2 enhances Cd tolerance, elevates Cd accumulation in roots and shoots, increases antioxidant capacity and GSH, and reduces oxidative damage; RNAi causes Cd sensitivity
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nucleobase-ascorbate transporter family; NAT signature motif; 11 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in leaves than stems and roots; in leaves mainly vascular bundles/phloem; in roots expressed broadly with stronger signal in vascular bundles; rapidly induced by Cd, peaking at 1 h
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in barley; RNAi silencing; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization with GFP; in situ PCR; Cd fluorescence imaging; Cd2+ flux measurements; RNA-seq; antioxidant enzyme assays; ICP-OES metabolite/ion analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvNAT2 is a plasma membrane NAT transporter targeted by Cd-responsive miR156g-3p_3. 2. HvNAT2 overexpression increased Cd tolerance despite higher Cd accumulation, associated with stronger antioxidant enzyme activities, higher GSH and lower MDA. 3. HvNAT2 RNAi reduced Cd tolerance and root Cd influx/accumulation. 4. Transcriptomics linked HvNAT2 function to redox, MAPK/ethylene signaling, glutathione metabolism and phytochelatin synthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering cadmium tolerance and potentially phytoremediation in barley and other plants; should be deployed with caution because overexpression also increases Cd accumulation in edible tissues, so use may be more suitable for remediation or combined with strategies limiting shoot/grain Cd.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher shoot and root Cd accumulation under overexpression may increase food-safety risk; altered mineral profiles (reduced concentration of some Fe/Cu/Mn/Zn traits on dry-weight basis) were observed.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq; RNA-seq; Supplementary Tables S4-S8, S10-S11; accession NA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter for overexpression and RNAi constructs
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: miR156g-3p_3; HMA3; ERD4; ACA9; GST3; GPX2; GPX25; GSTU6; GRXC6; AAO; Trx; ERF027; ACO1; PCS1; WRKY2; MYB; ADC2; MADS51
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in leaves than stems and roots; Cd inducible
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although annotated within the nucleobase-ascorbate transporter family, phylogeny/motif analysis suggested HvNAT2 likely transports oxidized purines/xanthine/uric acid rather than ascorbate directly. The metabolite effect is indirect via enhanced antioxidative capacity and detoxification pathways rather than direct biosynthesis of a nutrient metabolite.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Glutathione; phytochelatins; reactive oxygen species-scavenging antioxidant metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: thiol antioxidant; peptide chelator; redox-related metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cd-induced miR156g-3p_3 negatively regulates HvNAT2 and is implicated in Cd tolerance-associated redox regulation in barley
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream Cd-responsive regulator linked to the HvNAT2-mediated antioxidant and detoxification response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated by Cd in roots of Zhenong8 but unchanged in W6nk2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miRNA-seq; target prediction; qRT-PCR of miRNA; inverse expression relationship with HvNAT2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR156g-3p_3 was identified by miRNA sequencing as Cd responsive in contrasting barley genotypes. 2. HvNAT2 was predicted as its target, with cleavage at the 5' end of the transcript. 3. The miRNA is associated with the Cd-tolerance network upstream of HvNAT2.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct functional manipulation of miR156g-3p_3 was not reported; evidence is primarily sequencing, prediction, and expression correlation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq; RNA-seq; Supplementary Tables S4-S8, S10-S11; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely reduced HvNAT2 expression may compromise Cd tolerance, but direct miRNA transgenic tradeoff data were not reported.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Redox homeostasis and cadmium detoxification via control of transporter-mediated antioxidant capacity
